name: JR Plan
description: Decompose a plan into JR-style features, sequential tasks, and review handoffs.

fields.name: summary
fields.placeholder: What should be decomposed into features and tasks?

fields.name: plan_source
fields.description: Existing plan, issue, spec, or notes
fields.placeholder: Paste the plan or describe where to find it.

fields.name: review_policy
fields.description: Review expectations for generated tasks and features
fields.default: Every task gets code review; every feature gets architect review before human PR review.

steps.name: analyze
steps.prompt:
Analyze the supplied plan for JR-style orchestration.

Read relevant repo files and identify feature boundaries, dependency order, likely worktrees or branches, risk areas, and validation commands. Write findings to `.tickets/jr/{{ id }}/analysis.md`.

steps.name: decompose
steps.prompt:
Create a JR work decomposition in `.tickets/jr/{{ id }}/plan.md`.

Define JRFEAT entries for feature branches/worktrees and JRTASK entries ordered within each feature. Include parent feature ids, dependencies, expected files, validation commands, and reviewer role. Keep task scopes small enough for one focused implementation pass.

steps.name: verify
steps.prompt:
Review `.tickets/jr/{{ id }}/plan.md` for JR orchestration quality.

Ensure each feature has a clear branch/worktree target, tasks are sequential within a feature, cross-feature dependencies are explicit, and review gates are clear. Rewrite vague tasks. Do not leave hidden ordering assumptions.

on_reject.prompt:
The JR decomposition was rejected: {{ rejection_reason }}

Revise feature boundaries, task order, dependencies, or review gates.

steps.name: queue
steps.prompt:
Prepare the first ready JR work units.

Create or describe the initial JRFEAT and JRTASK tickets that have no unmet dependencies. Record the queue decision in `.tickets/jr/{{ id }}/queue.md` so future agents can resume deterministically.
